ck_baseline_candidate: none (softmax is elementwise reduction; no direct CK/rocBLAS baseline. PyTorch softmax torch.nn.functional.softmax serves as reference.)
notes: VERIFIED HARNESS: test_all() calls run_test(M=32768, N=8192, dtype="bf16") by default. Kernel entry: softmax_kernel (KERNEL_NAME constant at line 25). Launch fn: launch_softmax via build_softmax_module(). Timing: bench_gpu_us_torch() at line 94 records HIP events via torch.cuda.Event with warmup=10, iters=100. Prints FlyDSL GPU time at line 101: "[Perf] FlyDSL softmax gpu: {us:.1f} us". AIter optional via ROCDSL_COMPARE_AITER=1; imports aiter.ops.triton.softmax at line 172, runs on same M,N,dtype, prints "[Perf] AIter softmax gpu: {us:.1f} us" at line 184. Shape pinning: env var ROCDSL_SOFTMAX_SHAPES="M,N,dtype;..." overrides default 32768x8192xbf16. Arch: kernel is wave64 (CDNA via get_warp_size() in kernels_common.py line 74), targets gfx950 via MFMA through buffer copy atoms (BufferCopy128b/32b). No gfx1250/RDNA paths. Generic scalar path always used for arbitrary N. Bandwidth calculated at line 97 as (2*M*N*(4 if f32 else 2))/(time_s). Correctness verified vs torch.softmax() at line 66 with dtype-dependent tolerance (f32:1e-5, f16:1e-2, bf16:2e-2).

shape_env.ROCDSL_COMPARE_AITER: set to 1 to enable AIter timing (optional, for perf comparison)

metric_lines: Kernel avg time: {:.4f} ms via run_perftest

ck_baseline_candidate: none (RMSNorm is a reduction/normalization op, not GEMM; rocBLAS does not provide RMSNorm)
notes: SMALLER SHAPE RECOMMENDED FOR PROFILING: Default config uses M=32768, N=8192, bf16 which takes ~100ms per iteration (many JIT compilations on first run). Use ROCDSL_RMSNORM_SHAPES="1024,8192,bf16" to reduce to ~3ms per iteration. Kernel paths: (1) Fast path fires when N is multiple of (256*8)=2048 and bf16 uses vectorized 128b copy_atom; (2) generic path for arbitrary N uses scalar copy_atom. Default shape exercises fast path. BLOCK_THREADS=256, WARP_SIZE=64. Kernel entry fn: launch_rmsnorm (calls rmsnorm_kernel JIT). rocprofv3 kernel_include_regex: "rmsnorm.*". Printing: line 125 prints "[Perf] FlyDSL rmsnorm gpu: X.X us" when ROCDSL_COMPARE_AITER=1. AIter baseline via aiter.ops.triton.rmsnorm.rms_norm (imported at line 187). Verified pattern matches test_softmax.py. Clean output via 'ALL TESTS PASSED'. No multi-GPU or graph mode.

ck_baseline_candidate: CK LayerNorm (ck_launch_and_time baseline if available)
notes:
Kernel: 2-path block-wise LayerNorm (one block per row, 256 threads).
- Fast path (N == 8192): vectorized 128b BufferCopy via layout API, register caching, pipelined gamma/beta (lines 126-220).
- Generic path (arbitrary N): scalar 2-pass with 16b/32b BufferCopy (lines 221-298).

Entry point: `launch_layernorm(Input, Gamma, Beta, Output, m_in, stream)` (line 301-314 in kernels/layernorm_kernel.py).
Kernel name: `layernorm_kernel` (device @flyc.kernel at line 51).

Test harness (test_layernorm.py):
- __main__ -> test_all() (line 226-227).
- Shape config via ROCDSL_LAYERNORM_SHAPES env var; default (32768, 8192, bf16) commented out, uses large shape.
- Timing via run_perftest (HIP events), prints "Kernel avg time: X.XXXX ms", "Bandwidth: X.XX GB/s", "[Perf] FlyDSL layernorm gpu: X.X us" (lines 113-127).
- AIter comparable (aiter.ops.triton.norm.layer_norm) enabled via ROCDSL_COMPARE_AITER=1 (line 189, 200).

Smallest representative shape: 256x8192 bf16. Uses fast path (N = 256*8*4 = 8192), JITs quickly, prints perf. Bandwidth calc: (2*256*8192 + 2*8192)*2 = ~4.3 MB (fast), GPU time <1 ms typical.

No rocprofv3 kernel_include_regex needed (kernel name "layernorm_kernel" is straightforward).
Target: MI350X gfx950, bf16 datatype, MFMA-based reductions.

ck_baseline_candidate: vLLM::topk_gating_softmax (reference: softmax + topk + renormalization)
notes: Single-kernel, correctness-gated MoE gating operator (softmax + top-K selection + optional renormalization). Default test_all() runs 4 shapes: (1024x128xk6, bf16), (512x64xk2, bf16), (256x8xk2, f32), (128x128xk6, f16). For fastest JIT + perf capture, pin smallest shape with ROCDSL_TOPK_GATING_SHAPES=256,8,2,f32; at 38 KB total working set, JIT will be sub-second. Kernel is pure CDNA wave64 (no RDNA path), uses sub-warp butterfly reductions with no shared memory. Outputs printed: (1) per-config correctness pass/fail; (2) indices match % vs torch.topk (tolerant of K-th boundary ties in bf16/f16); (3) weight max error (sorted); (4) token_expert_indices validity; (5) renormalization sum error; (6) GPU timing via torch.cuda.Event if HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES set. AIter comparable field is None because test does not wire AIter comparison (only FlyDSL GPU timing via bench_gpu_us_torch when ROCDSL_COMPARE_AITER=1, but aiter_gpu_us stays None in perf table). CK baseline would be vLLM's topk_gating_softmax kernel (used in MoE router inference pipelines). rocprofv3 kernel name hint: search for "topk_gating_softmax_kernel" in traces.

ck_baseline_candidate: none
notes: Per-token quantization kernel: per-row max|x| find, scale = max_abs/127, output[i] = fptosi(x[i]/scale). Layout API with buffer-backed tensors, copy atoms for 128b f16 reads (8xf16) and 64b i8 writes (8xi8). Wave64 reduction (CDNA gfx950). Prints: "Bandwidth: X GB/s" and details in benchmark_per_token_quant(). AIter baseline (per_token_quant_hip) available when HAS_AITER=True (try-import aiter.ops.quant). Config output shows M/N/block_size/loops/memory_traffic. To enable test pass FLYDSL_RUN_QUANT=1. Timings via run_perftest returns (data, avg_us); avg_us prints via get_trace_perf. Default shapes M=4096, N=8192; override with -m/-n or --multi-test. Arch-agnostic (uses get_rocm_arch() for wave_size detection). No rocprofv3 kernel regex hint needed—kernel name is quant_kernel. Benchmark also runs correctness check on output_diff and scale_diff vs reference.

ck_baseline_candidate: false
cli_flags: --all-models (opt-in for cross-model sweep; default runs GPT-OSS TP8)
metric_lines: [Perf] FlyDSL softmax gpu: \d+\.\d+ us (note: code prints 'fly_us', 'aiter_us', 'speedup' keys to errs dict)
[Perf].*speedup=\d+\.\d+x (AIter comparison when AITER_REPO set or aiter installed)
notes: RECIPE: (A) Default invocation runs 1 representative model (GPT-OSS TP8: 8 Q-heads, 1 KV-head, D=64) with T=1/32/128 (decode/prefill), flash+nonflash layouts, across 6 categories (decode, prefill, nonflash, pos_dtype i32/i64, reuse_freqs, fp8 cache). Each category prints [PASS/FAIL] with q/k/kc/vc max errors and optional benchmark timings (fly_us, aiter_us, speedup). (B) FLYDSL_BENCH=1 enables GPU timing via _bench_gpu_us() (20 warmup, 200 iters, torch.cuda.Event), prints fly_us and aiter_us only when HAS_AITER=True (requires AITER_REPO env var or aiter package). (C) Single-kernel config: head_dim=64, num_q_heads=8, num_kv_heads=1, num_tokens=1 (smallest/fastest JIT), flash_layout=True, dtype_str='bf16', apply_scale=False, reuse_freqs_front_part=True, pos_dtype='i32', block_size=16, max_pos=8192. (D) Grid: (max(8,1)=8, 1, 1) threads, block (WARP_SIZE=64 on gfx950, 1, 1). Kernel launch via _jit_launch_fused_rope_cache -> fused_qk_rope_reshape_and_cache. (E) JIT config: fused_rope_cache_kernel.build_fused_rope_cache_module(head_dim=64, num_q_heads=8, num_kv_heads=1, block_size=16, flash_layout=True, dtype_str='bf16', apply_scale=False, reuse_freqs_front_part=True, pos_dtype='i32'). (F) AIter comparable: aiter.ops.triton.fusions.fused_kv_cache.fused_qk_rope_reshape_and_cache (Triton kernel). (G) Timing output captured in run_test() errs dict: keys 'fly_us' (FlyDSL µs), 'aiter_us' (AIter µs), 'speedup' (ratio). (H) No CK/rocBLAS direct baseline—this is a fused rope+cache op (unique to attention), not a standalone matrix multiply. AIter Triton is the primary cross-check. (I) SMALLEST SHAPE (T=1, all D=64, single token): jits to ~1-2s, runs in <1µs; prefill (T=128) may be 10-100x slower. (J) Correctness: max 10 tests per config, fp8 path skipped on non-CDNA (gfx10xx/gfx11xx/gfx12xx). Target gfx950 has zero-sized fp8 quantization (e4m3fn) and MFMA MMA atoms; respects 160KB LDS. (K) Architecture key: gfx950 is CDNA4 (wave64), VEC_WIDTH auto-tuned to ceil(D/64) (=1 for D=64), vecs_per_head=64, BLOCK_THREADS=64. No RDNA skips. Entry: launch_fused_rope_cache wraps _jit_launch_fused_rope_cache + mark_layout_dynamic for dynamic T.

aiter_comparable: torch.ops.aiter.pa_decode_gluon (Gluon reference paged-attention kernel; invoked via run_gluon_ps() at test_pa.py:802–823)
ck_baseline_candidate: No CK baseline available; use AIter Gluon pa_decode_gluon as reference. For paged-attention performance comparison, AIter uses Triton Gluon compiled at runtime.
kernel_name_hint: pa_decode_ps_kernel (launch grid=(num_sm, 1, TILES_PER_BLOCK), block=(BLOCK_THREADS, 1, 1) at pa_decode_fp8.py:1708). PS mode uses dynamic work distribution via compile_pa_decode_ps(). For kernel capture, filter: 'pa_decode.*kernel|pa_decode.*launch'
notes:
CRITICAL: This is test_pa.py's __main__ entry point (normal_accuracy_test → sliding_window_accuracy_test). The harness is a REGRESSION TEST that compares FlyDSL PS (persistent scheduling) PA decode to:
1. Torch reference (reference_masked_attention + torch_mha_extend) — correctness baseline.
2. AIter Gluon pa_decode_gluon (if HAS_GLUON=True) — performance comparison.

KERNELS: Two main FlyDSL kernel implementations:
- ps_normal: compile_pa_decode_ps() → pa_decode_ps_kernel (persistent scheduling; normal/sliding-window phase)
- ps_reduce: compile_pa_decode_sw_reduce() (reduce phase for sliding-window mode).

TIMING: measure_us() uses torch.cuda.Event with 3 warmups + 10 iters; CUDA graph capture attempted if use_cuda_graph=True.
Output format: "us_flydsl_ps: float (microseconds)"; csv saved to run_pa_decode_ps_test.ps_normal_accuracy.block_size_1024.triton.*.csv

SMALLEST SHAPE (for fastest JIT): query_length=1, batch_size=3, context_length=1027, num_heads=(8,1), head_dim=128, block_size=1024.
Scaling up: For larger shapes, increase batch_size (3→81), context_length (1027→8192), or query_length (1→4).
Caution: Large context (8192) + batch (128) with sliding_window > 0 runs multiple kernels (encode + reduce); budget ~20-60 GB/s throughput for full test suite.

PRECISION: fp8 (quantized K/V) + bf16 (query/output). Per-token quantization (smaller scales tensor). Per-tensor is faster but lower precision.

VALIDATION: Test PASSES if err_flydsl_ps == 0 AND all diffs <= tolerance (see get_tolerance()). Set --sample_rate=1.0 or lower to control test case count.

ARCH: gfx950 only (uses MFMA, MI350X). Will JIT fail if ARCH != gfx950 in build or device; no special gfx1250/RDNA branch in pa_decode_ps_kernel, but uses get_hip_arch() from flydsl.runtime.device.

ENV: Set PYTHONPATH=/sgl-workspace/FlyDSL-lab/build-fly/python_packages:/sgl-workspace/FlyDSL-lab before running. No ROCDSL_*_SHAPES env vars (uses argparse). JIT cache: FLYDSL_RUNTIME_ENABLE_CACHE=1 recommended for graph capture.

ck_baseline_candidate: hipBLASLt-based paged attention if available; else DeepSeek-V3 reference attention
notes: BLOCK_N=64 single-tile on gfx950 (CDNA4). Kernel: kn_mla_fwd_decode_m16x8_fp8_fp8 (lines 309–2070), launched via @flyc.jit launch_mla_fwd_decode_m16x8_fp8_fp8 (lines 2072–2105). JIT: @flyc.kernel decorator, MLIR compilation. Timing: run_perftest() in test_common.py (line 149) uses torch.cuda HIP events, runs warmup=20 iters, then num_iters=100, repeats=3 times; filters outliers via IQR, reports median us_p50 and range. Perf output: "TFLOPS={flops / us / 1e6:.2f} TB/s={bw / us / 1e6:.2f} us_p50={us:.2f} us_range=[min_us, max_us]" (lines 400–403). AIter benchmarks available via --bench_aiter flag (lines 407–467, 494). Shapes: hardcoded to batch=1, ctx_len=64 (single KV tile, BLOCK_N=64). All sizes fit gfx950 LDS budget (V3_TOTAL_LDS_BYTES=84736 bytes). No env-var shape override; use CLI args. Expected TFLOPS ~60–80, TB/s ~100–150 for MI350X at clock ~2.5 GHz.
metric_lines: cos_diff=.*  TFLOPS=.*  TB/s=.*  err_ratio=.*  us_p50=.*  us_range=

aiter_comparable: aiter.mha_fwd (CK backend) or aiter.fmha_v3_fwd (ASM backend, bf16 only)
ck_baseline_candidate: aiter.mha_fwd with softmax_scale=1/sqrt(head_dim), causal=True
metric_lines: [Perf] FlyDSL flash_attn_func gpu: <time> us (from run_perftest profiler)
notes: Kernel timing: run_perftest returns (data, us) where us is microseconds from torch.profiler device_time_sum. Test harness calculates TFLOPS as flops / (us * 1e-6) / 1e12 where flops = 4.0*S*s_eff*D*H*B and s_eff = S/2 (causal) or S (non-causal). Smallest recommended shape is (1,128,8,128) to minimize JIT compile time while remaining representative of attention workload. Shape constraints: seq_len % 128 == 0, head_dim % 32 == 0 and >= 64. Test supports --causal / --no-causal flags; default runs both causal and non-causal. AIter comparison available with --compare flag (requires aiter package). Kernel JIT compiles on first invocation with build_flash_attn_func_module; uses custom LLVM tools if available (gfx950+ optimized path). Device kernel flash_attn_func_kernel defined at @flyc.kernel in flash_attn_func.py:257. Kernel entry: build_flash_attn_func_module returns exe callable with signature exe(Q_flat, K_flat, V_flat, O_flat, batch_size, seq_len). Expected kernel name in rocprofv3 kernel_include_regex: kernel substring contains 'flash_attn_func' or 'amdgcn.kernel' target. For gfx950 specifically: uses MFMA32 (K=16), HW transpose LDS read (ds_read_tr16_b64), 160KB LDS. Verify with HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 to ensure single GPU capture.

notes: SMALLEST gfx950 param set (32x384x7168 GEMM) for fast JIT. Target kernel: hgemm_bf16_32x64x256_W1x2x2_S2_BT_BLDS1_AS1_SPK14 (SPLIT_K=14, ASYNC_COPY=True via gfx950 path, B_TO_LDS=True). Grid=(6,14,1), Block=(256,1,1). Test runs: (1) torch.mm baseline via run_perftest with HIP events, (2) hgemm_splitk_ kernel launch, reports GPU timing in microseconds. Prints: "[flyc] Throughput: us, TFLOPS, BW TB/s, Torch reference, speedup ratio". Perf extraction: parse "Throughput:" line for `us` (microseconds), `tflops` (TFLOPS), `tbps` (TB/s), `Speedup` ratio. No rocprofv3 needed — kernel perf via HIP event timing inside run_perftest. Correctness verified via torch.mm f32 reference at line 128. PYTHONPATH REQUIRED: /sgl-workspace/FlyDSL-lab/build-fly/python_packages:/sgl-workspace/FlyDSL-lab. Command runs as __main__ (pytest skip decorator bypassed).

notes: Entry point: __main__ at line 445. Function: test_mfma_a8_flyc_preshuffle (line 91) for fp8/int8/bf16 activations; test_mfma_w4_flyc_preshuffle (line 304) for MXFP4 (gfx950-only). Kernel: @flyc.kernel kernel_gemm (line 313 in kernels/preshuffle_gemm.py) + @flyc.jit launch_gemm (line 777) which compiles and launches with wave64 MFMA. JIT compiles in-memory; no persistent .o file. Verified on MI350X (gfx950) only. Default runs a8w8 preshuffle fp8 GEMM; --wfp4 flag enables gfx950-exclusive MXFP4 variant. Layout API-based preshuffle GEMM with double-buffered B, hot_loop_scheduler for gfx950 MFMA. Timing via HIP events in _bench_kernel() using torch.cuda.Event. ck_baseline_candidate: rocBLAS fp8_mm or CK scaled_gemm for FP8 with row-wise scales
notes: FP8 row-scale GEMM with two variants: 4-wave (BLOCK_M/N=64-256, uses XCD swizzle for load balancing) and 8-wave (BLOCK_M>=128, BLOCK_N>=256). For 4-wave use --tile_m 64 --tile_n 64 (smallest valid); for 8-wave use --tile_m 128 --tile_n 256. AIter baseline: torch._scaled_mm (use --vs_torch). JIT caches on K, BLOCK_M, BLOCK_N, use_xcd_remap, b_preshuffled params. Single-shape invocation: M=512, N=2112, K=7168 (first parametrized test case) avoids large 8192^3 which JITs slowly. Validation: verify_output checks output correctness (rtol/atol=0.1).

Multi-dtype support (fp8/fp16/bf16/int8/int4/int4_bf16/fp4/a8w4) but default is fp8 for fastest JIT. Stage1 uses SiLU gate; doweight_stage1 toggles gate*weight routing. CLI invocation via __main__ argparse (--tokens, --topk, --model_dim, --dtype choices=[f16,bf16,f32], --num_iters, --num_warmup). Correctness verified via torch.sum comparison. Runs standalone without aiter (no ROCDSL_COMPARE_AITER env var wiring in this test); torch.sum serves as reference. Can be extended per test_softmax.py pattern to add ROCDSL_MOE_REDUCE_SHAPES env var and bench_gpu_us_torch if AIter MoE reduce is available.

ck_baseline_candidate: none
notes: Multi-GPU distributed test (requires 2+ GPUs). Spawns torchrun-style process group with mp.spawn(). Kernel sources in kernels/custom_all_reduce_kernel.py; entry launchers are @flyc.jit wrappers (run_1stage_arr, run_2stage_arr, run_2stage_write_mode) that auto-compile via make_allreduce_kernels(N, dtype_str, world_size, threads). Timing via torch.cuda.Event (prints avg_time_us microseconds per iteration). Smallest shape (2,7168,bf16) uses 1-stage path, JITs fast (~14K elem). AIter comparison available via FLYDSL_AITER_IMPL=aiter env var. To profile single kernel manually: set PYTHONPATH, init dist w/ MASTER_ADDR/MASTER_PORT, instantiate FlyDSLAllreduce, call custom_all_reduce(). No rocprofv3 kernel names extractable—kernels are named run_*stage_* at launch, generated kernel symbols baked at compile time by @flyc.kernel DSL.

ck_baseline_candidate: Not applicable (vector add is elementwise; rocBLAS/CK target GEMM/reduction)
notes: **Kernel entry**: vecAddKernel (device kernel, registered via @flyc.kernel decorator); vecAdd (JIT launch wrapper). **Memory op**: elementwise ADD, 3 arrays (A, B, C), float32, 4-byte elements. **Default shape**: THREADS_PER_BLOCK=256, VEC_WIDTH=4, TILE_ELEMS=1024, SIZE=1024*10000=10.24M elements = 122 MB total traffic. **Timing method**: torch.cuda.Event HIP events in benchmark_pytorch_add() (lines 98-104) and kernel_launch() (lines 152-156); run_perftest() wrapper with num_iters=20, num_warmup=2. **Perf print pattern**: Line 170 outputs "[FlyDSL kernel: X.XXXX ms, Bandwidth: X.XX GB/s]" via avg_ms and bandwidth_gbs. **Baseline included**: benchmark_pytorch_add() at line 89 benchmarks torch.add on same SIZE, outputs PyTorch bandwidth for comparison (line 175-176). **AIter**: No AIter integration yet; torch.add is primary baseline. **Shape downsize option**: Modify SIZE calculation by reducing multiplier (10000 -> 100 gives 1.2 MB traffic, 100x faster JIT). Alternatively, pass --vec-width to adjust tile size. **Arch**: gfx950 (CDNA4, 64-wide wave, no arch guards in test file, works natively). **Correctness**: checkAllclose() at line 149 validates against torch.add result before perf runs.

aiter_comparable: N/A (multi-PE shmem collective, no single-GPU AIter equivalent)
ck_baseline_candidate: N/A (communication operation; compare against native HIP mi shmem)
notes: This test IS CORRECTNESS-ONLY and requires multi-GPU (2+ PEs) via torchrun. It validates FlyDSL shmem integration with mori (shared memory collective library) via two kernels: (1) shmem_basic_kernel: calls mori_shmem_my_pe() and mori_shmem_n_pes(), writes results to device output buffer; (2) shmem_put_kernel: calls mori_shmem_int32_p() to perform a symmetric put to peer PE, then mori_shmem_quiet_thread(). No GPU-time metrics are printed. The @flyc.kernel DSL kernels use link_extern + FFI to invoke mori bitcode symbols (ffi module_init_fn pattern). Launch is via @flyc.jit wrappers (launch_basic, launch_put) with 1x1x1 grid/block. Benchmark would require: (a) embedding timing inside run_basic/run_put before/after torch.cuda.synchronize() pairs using torch.cuda.Event(enable_timing=True) or HIP events, (b) extracting launch_basic/launch_put calls into a separate bench function accepting count+iters, (c) using bench_gpu_us_torch pattern from benchmark_common.py. Test depends on mori package + 2 physical GPUs + torch.distributed. Single-GPU invocation will fail import. Entry point: main() → setup_distributed() → run_basic/run_put. To enable profiling as a future recipe: add HIP event timing around kernel launches, env var BENCH_SHMEM_ITERS to control iteration count, and extract individual PE comm latencies.
